user_query:
We're a seed-stage SaaS fintech and need a compact ops bundle for this month. Please read all the following inputs from input/:
- input/mrr.csv — our monthly MRR components for the current month
- input/metrics_context.txt — short context about our model/stage to reflect in the update
- input/banking_case.txt — a customer-facing payment delay scenario with jurisdiction and account context
- input/chords.txt — a simple chord progression we want to share internally

Then produce these deliverables under output/:
1) output/investor_update.md — Include a single top YAML block with keys exactly: metrics: { arr: <integer dollars>, mrr: <integer dollars>, ndr: "<integer percent>%" } plus a short TL;DR section. Compute mrr as ending MRR for the month, arr = mrr*12, and ndr = (Starting_MRR + Expansion - Contraction - Churn) / Starting_MRR expressed as a percentage rounded to the nearest whole number with a trailing % sign. Base all numbers strictly on input/mrr.csv. Keep arr and mrr as plain integers in dollars (no commas or $), and ndr with the % sign.
2) output/customer_update.txt — Draft a clear, compliant customer update for the payment delay scenario following a control-first mindset. It must include the three labeled lines exactly once each: "Current status:", "Next step:", and "Expected update:" with concise content on the same lines. Avoid absolute promises and legal conclusions.
3) output/chords.md — Generate ASCII guitar chord diagrams for every chord listed (in order) in input/chords.txt. Include a legend explaining symbols and a labeled section for each chord. Use text-only characters to show open strings (◯), muted strings (✕), and fretted positions (●) with a simple fretboard grid. Keep everything in plain text.
4) output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md — Create at least two well-structured entries using the logging format with IDs like [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX]. One must be a knowledge_gap and one a best_practice discovered while doing this task. Include: Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, and Metadata (with Tags and, if applicable, Pattern-Key and See Also). Mark one that you later promote as promoted in its Status with a note about where it was promoted.
5) output/.learnings/ERRORS.md — Add at least one error entry [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX] capturing a plausible failure or near-miss encountered/anticipated in this workflow, with Summary, Error block, Context, Suggested Fix, and Metadata.
6) output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md — Add at least one feature request entry [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX] that would have made this work smoother. Include Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, and Metadata.
7) output/CLAUDE.md — Promote one high-value learning into a concise, durable rule or two (1–4 lines) for future sessions. Reference the promoted learning ID and keep the rule phrased as a prevention guideline for future work.

Please keep all outputs text-only and ensure paths use output/ and .learnings/ as specified.
